scenario_description: A user presents eight teddy bears in a circle, noting one has a patched ear. The user then rearranges them into two rows of four, mixing their positions. The final question asks for the specific location of the patched bear in the new arrangement.

turns.text: Here are my eight teddy bears sitting in a circle on the rug. One of them has a little brown patch on its left ear. Can you spot which one it is?
turns.image_description: Top-down view of eight distinct teddy bears arranged in a perfect circle on a beige rug. Clockwise from the top: 1) A large brown bear with no accessories. 2) A small grey bear wearing a red bow tie. 3) A medium white bear with a blue ribbon around its neck. 4) A black bear holding a tiny honey pot. 5) A golden-brown bear with a visible triangular brown fabric patch stitched onto its left ear. 6) A dark chocolate bear with a green hat. 7) A light tan bear with a yellow scarf. 8) A speckled grey bear with no accessories. The lighting is soft and even.

turns.text: I see the golden-brown bear at the bottom-right of the circle has that distinctive patch on its left ear. It stands out against the others!
Thanks! I just moved them all around to make two neat rows of four. Here is the new setup.
turns.image_description: The same eight teddy bears are now arranged in two horizontal rows of four on the same beige rug. Top row (left to right): 1) The small grey bear with the red bow tie. 2) The black bear holding the honey pot. 3) The light tan bear with the yellow scarf. 4) The large brown bear with no accessories. Bottom row (left to right): 1) The speckled grey bear. 2) The dark chocolate bear with the green hat. 3) The golden-brown bear with the brown patch on its left ear. 4) The medium white bear with the blue ribbon. The bears are facing forward.

turns.text: That looks much more organized! They seem pretty mixed up compared to the circle. Did you keep the one with the patch near the edge?

Exactly. Now, looking at this second picture, where is the bear with the patched ear? A) Top row, third from left B) Bottom row, third from left C) Bottom row, second from left D) Top row, fourth from left

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: In Turn 1, the image identifies the target entity as the 'golden-brown bear with a brown patch on its left ear'. In Turn 3, the image shows the new arrangement. By visually tracking the unique features of the target entity (golden-brown fur + patch on left ear), we locate it in the bottom row. Counting from the left in the bottom row: 1st is speckled grey, 2nd is dark chocolate, 3rd is the golden-brown bear with the patch. Therefore, the position is Bottom row, third from left.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The model must link the description of the 'patched ear' from the first turn to the specific visual instance of the golden-brown bear in the second turn, while ignoring other bears that might look similar or be in confusing positions. A model relying only on the last image without recognizing the specific bear described earlier might guess based on proximity to other objects or fail to distinguish the specific bear if there were multiple brown ones (though here the patch is unique, the task requires cross-turn entity re-identification).

_meta.scenario: A group of eight teddy bears is sitting in a circle; one bear has a patch on its ear. The user rearranges the bears into two rows of four, mixing their positions completely. The user then asks for the position (e.g., 'top row, second from left') of the bear with the patched ear.
